body:
## Why this matters

The homelab research showed that media and NAS validation will hit a **shared-storage / RWX blocker** unless the gap is made explicit. If we do not surface that dependency now, later service and restore issues will silently assume a storage model the lab may not actually provide.

## Scope

Define the first shared-storage blocker issue under #52:

- state where NAS/media validation depends on RWX or shared-media assumptions
- distinguish what the current lab can validate with existing storage from what needs a blocker or separate lane
- define the minimum evidence needed before a shared-storage assumption can be treated as valid
- link the impact on service-catalog and backup/restore work directly

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The shared-storage / RWX blocker is explicit and linked to the relevant role and service lanes
- [ ] The issue states what can proceed on current storage assumptions and what cannot
- [ ] The minimum evidence needed to unblock shared-storage expectations is documented
- [ ] The issue is linked to #52 and referenced by the workload/restore lanes it affects

## Links

- Child of #52
- Blocks parts of #51 and #55
- Derived from the storage gap surfaced while mining `projectbluefin/bluespeed#7` and `#9`
title: feat: surface shared-storage and RWX blocker for homelab validation
